chosen.headline: Nifty IT plunges ~2.7% to a three-year low; TCS, Infosys, HCLTech hit 52-week lows

story.headline: Nifty IT plunges ~2.7% to a three-year low; TCS, Infosys, HCLTech hit 52-week lows
story.what_happened: The Nifty IT index sank about 2.7% on June 30, sharply underperforming the Nifty 50 (-0.34%) and extending its losing streak to a third straight session (down roughly 4.6% over three days). Heavyweights led the slide: Infosys fell ~3.5%, TCS ~3.2%, Wipro ~2.9%, HCLTech ~2.8% and Tech Mahindra ~2%, with several IT names hitting 52-week lows and the index touching a three-year low. The selloff was driven by fears that US interest rates stay higher-for-longer (pressuring discretionary tech spend) and worries about AI-driven disruption to the IT services model.
story.why_it_matters: IT is among the most heavily weighted sectors in Indian benchmarks and is highly export-/US-demand-sensitive. A move to a three-year low with multiple 52-week lows signals deepening pessimism on US discretionary tech budgets and the AI-disruption overhang for traditional services revenue - the single biggest drag on the headline indices today.
key_numbers: Infosys ~-3.5%, TCS ~-3.2%, Wipro ~-2.9%, HCLTech ~-2.8%, Tech Mahindra ~-2%
Index at ~3-year low; multiple constituents at 52-week lows
story.investor_takeaway: For educational context only: the IT drawdown is tied to macro (US rate outlook) and structural (AI-disruption) concerns rather than a single company event - a sector-wide de-rating worth monitoring. Not investment advice.

sources.name: Moneycontrol via TradingView - Nifty IT falls nearly 3%
Business Standard - Nifty IT sinks to 3-year low; TCS, Infosys, HCL Tech hit 52-wk lows
